headline: 29 May 2026
subtitle: MOU Draft Unsigned; IRGC Alert Posture Sustained; AQI Very Unhealthy

metrics.metric: Days since last confirmed cyber incident (Gulf FS)

items.bold_heading: No new sirens during coverage window
items.text: No new sirens or civil defense emergency broadcasts reported in Kuwait during the coverage window (28 May 07:00 – 29 May 07:00 UTC+3). The IRGC aerial attack and nationwide sirens of 28 May (~04:50 UTC+3) were covered in Report #79; elevated alert posture remained in effect through the coverage period; no formal all-clear issued at compilation time.

items.bold_heading: Kuwait MFA formal condemnation — 28 May
items.text: Kuwait Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned the IRGC attack and stated Kuwait 'reserves the right to take any and all necessary measures to defend its territory, sovereignty and security.' This is Kuwait's strongest diplomatic statement since the conflict began.

items.bold_heading: No new confirmed kinetic strikes on Kuwait during coverage window
items.text: No confirmed follow-on strikes on Kuwait during the daytime/overnight portion of 28 May (07:00 UTC+3 onwards). Primary IRGC aerial attack (~04:50 UTC+3, 28 May) was covered in Report #79.

items.bold_heading: IRGC warning shots at 4 vessels near Strait of Hormuz — 28 May, ~00:35 UTC+3 [SINGLE SOURCE]
items.text: IRGC Navy fired warning shots at four vessels attempting to transit the Strait without prior IRGC coordination; ships withdrew after shots were fired. Event is at the window boundary (00:35 UTC+3) — included for maritime risk completeness.

items.bold_heading: IRGC claims oversight of 26 vessel passages through Hormuz [SINGLE SOURCE]
items.text: IRGC Navy stated it oversaw passage of 26 vessels through the Strait under its coordination framework in the prior 24 hours, warning of a 'decisive response' to any disruption. A de facto IRGC authorization requirement persists.

items.bold_heading: US-Iran mutual ceasefire violation accusations; kinetic exchange of 28 May confirmed by both sides
items.text: US and Iran trade strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire terms; each side uses the other's kinetic actions as justification for its own. No new US strikes confirmed during the coverage window.

items.bold_heading: Eid Al-Adha Day 4 (29 May) — last day of private sector holiday
items.text: Kuwait private sector Eid holiday runs 26–29 May; public sector through 31 May. Banks, government, and most private companies closed on 29 May. Full private sector resumption 1 June 2026; public sector 1 June.

items.bold_heading: KWI: Partial operations (T4/T5) continued; T1 June 1 reopening provisional
items.text: Terminals 4 (Kuwait Airways) and 5 (Jazeera Airways) remained operational on 28 May despite IRGC attack on airport area. CAA has not revised T1 June 1 statement as of compilation — treat June 1 T1 as provisional pending post-attack safety assessment; do not finalise post-Eid travel before CAA confirmation.

items.bold_heading: Kuwait AQI: PM2.5 = 225 (Very Unhealthy) — shamal dust storm conditions likely [TIMESTAMP UNCONFIRMED]
items.text: General Kuwait City monitoring shows PM2.5 AQI of 225 (Very Unhealthy) and PM10 AQI of 356 (Hazardous). Staff advisory: avoid unnecessary outdoor exposure; monitor AQI before 1 June return to offices.
sources: aqi.in general Kuwait City station, 29 May 2026

items.bold_heading: Shuwaikh Port: Elevated alert status probable; operational status unconfirmed
items.text: May 28 IRGC aerial campaign likely prompted precautionary upgrade to Security Level 3 at Shuwaikh; daylight-only restriction (05:00–19:00 UTC+3) and Sahel app QR requirement maintained. No confirmed update during coverage window.

items.bold_heading: Land borders: Al-Nuwaisib (Saudi), Abdali (Iraq) — elevated closure risk; Eid return traffic
items.text: Post-IRGC attack period sustains short-notice closure risk at Abdali; Eid return traffic increases pressure on Saudi crossing from 30 May. No confirmed closures during coverage window.

items.bold_heading: MOU draft agreed but unsigned — Trump 'wants a couple of days'; JD Vance 'TBD'
items.text: US and Iranian negotiators finalised a 60-day MOU framework on 28 May (ceasefire extension, Hormuz de-mining in 30 days, Iran oil sanctions waivers, Lebanon war termination), but Trump has not approved. Both sides publicly contradict each other on Hormuz control terms.

items.bold_heading: Oil: Brent ~$93.50 on MOU non-approval; dual-scenario window open
items.text: Markets partially priced in Hormuz reopening on MOU news, then reversed on Trump non-approval. Dual scenario: MOU signed within 72h → Brent $85–88; MOU rejected → Brent $100–110+.

items.bold_heading: Gulf states accelerating defense diversification; GCC hedging US security umbrella
items.text: UAE signed a defense cooperation agreement with France; Qatar signed an MOU with Canada on security cooperation — GCC governments hedge against US withdrawal regardless of MOU outcome.

items.bold_heading: Compliance restart: deferred actions must be prioritised before 09:00 UTC+3 on 1 June
items.text: SAR filings deferred under FIN-2026-Alert002 (IRGC shadow fleet), cybersecurity patches, and OFAC screening queue must be actioned before normal operations resume on 1 June; confirm all operational units are reachable before holiday ends.

items.bold_heading: Oil price dual-scenario: Trump MOU decision within ~72h is the pivotal variable
items.text: MOU signed → Brent toward $85–88 (collateral revaluation, margin reduction). MOU rejected → Brent $100–110+ (margin calls, SWF stress). Dual-scenario position review required over the weekend.

items.bold_heading: Staff safety: PM2.5 = 225 (Very Unhealthy) + elevated security posture — outdoor advisory
items.text: Advise staff to minimise outdoor exposure on 29 May and monitor AQI before returning to offices on 1 June; severe dust conditions compound the security-related movement advisory from May 28.

items.bold_heading: IRGC kinetic mode sustained — elevated SOC and DDoS posture required through 31 May
items.text: May 28 IRGC attack on Kuwait and ongoing US-Iran kinetic exchanges sustain the pro-Iran hacktivist retaliation window. Active groups: 313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet, Fatimion. SOC on-call contacts must be confirmed reachable before 1 June resumption.

items.bold_heading: KWI T1 June 1 opening — treat as provisional; await CAA confirmation by 30 May
items.text: KWI attacked on 28 May; CAA expected to issue a T1 status statement by 30 May. If no confirmation by end of 30 May, treat June 1 T1 as delayed and adjust all June 1–3 business travel schedules.

items.bold_heading: JWC JWLA-033 unchanged — WRP 1–3%; May 28 IRGC attack forecloses near-term de-listing
items.text: No JWC de-listing of Kuwait or other Gulf Listed Areas warranted after the 28 May IRGC attack. WRP for Gulf transits remains 1–3% of vessel hull value per voyage; US/UK/Israeli-flagged vessels at approximately 3× standard rate. EASA CZIB renewal for Kuwait airspace expected imminently.

items.bold_heading: MOU mine-clearance provision (30 days post-signature): no WRP reduction warranted until Trump signs
items.text: Draft MOU commits Iran to demine Hormuz within 30 days of signature. No AWRP or voyage premium reduction should be modelled before formal signature and independent verification. Minimum VLCC voyage premium remains ~$2.5M; JMIC Hormuz risk unchanged at CRITICAL.

items.bold_heading: IRGC warning shots at 4 vessels — P&I and H&M policies must explicitly cover IRGC hostile-action [SINGLE SOURCE]
items.text: May 28 Hormuz warning shots demonstrate IRGC will intercept unauthorised vessels. Standard war-risk policies may exclude IRGC-directed hostile actions if not specifically named. Review all Gulf-trade counterparty insurance for IRGC exclusion clauses.

articles.title: Groundhog Day: Iran War Edition
articles.author: Author unconfirmed
articles.summary: Examines the repetitive cycle of US-Iran negotiations where deal announcements fail to materialise, punctuated by renewed kinetic exchanges; Trump is characterised as unpredictable, not satisfied with terms, refusing to rush, and adding new conditions including Abraham Accords expansion requirements. The 60-day MOU draft may represent another iteration of the same structural impasse in the negotiations cycle.
articles.relevance: Confirms that oil price volatility, compliance uncertainty, and IRGC kinetic/cyber threat posture cannot be structurally resolved on a predictable timeline — MOU failure must be treated as a base-case possibility, not a tail risk.

articles.title: Persian Gulf Countries, From UAE to Saudi Arabia, Know Iran Won the War
articles.author: Amir Handjani
articles.summary: GCC governments are calculating that Iran's regional power instruments (Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi proxies) were degraded but not destroyed, and are hedging by forming new defense partnerships (UAE-France, Qatar-Canada) while adopting a more conditional, self-interested foreign policy. The article describes the Iran war as the most disorienting regional event since the 1979 revolution.
articles.relevance: Structural fragmentation of the US security umbrella in the Gulf elevates the long-term political risk baseline for Kuwait-based operations regardless of MOU outcome; country-risk models should be recalibrated for a multipolar Gulf security environment.

items.bold_heading: Pro-Iran hacktivist retaliation window sustained through 31 May — elevated SOC posture required
items.text: IRGC kinetic attack on Kuwait (28 May) and ongoing US-Iran mutual kinetic exchanges sustain a 72h+ elevated hacktivist threat window. Active groups: 313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet, Fatimion. GCC financial services is the #2 most targeted sector (144+ verified incidents per SocRadar). No new CVEs.
items.threat_actor: IRGC-affiliated hacktivists (313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet, Fatimion)
items.recommended_action: Maintain elevated SOC posture through 31 May; confirm DDoS scrubbing, WAF, and CDN at full capacity; verify on-call contacts reachable before 1 June resumption.

items.bold_heading: APT34/MuddyWater: SWIFT credential theft and payment gateway web shell — Eid gap closes 1 June
items.text: Active FINRA advisory (March–May 2026) documents APT34 (OilRig) and MuddyWater targeting SWIFT messaging credentials and inserting web shells on payment gateways; Eid staffing gap (26–29 May) creates a detection window for intrusions initiated during holiday. No applicable CVEs.
items.recommended_action: Audit SWIFT CSP controls and review payment gateway access logs for the Eid period before 09:00 UTC+3 on 1 June; confirm privileged account MFA is active.

items.bold_heading: UAE warning: Iran deploying AI tools (ChatGPT) to engineer Gulf cyberattacks
items.text: UAE Cybersecurity Council Chairman warns Iran is using AI tools including ChatGPT to engineer attacks against Gulf financial, aviation, and law enforcement platforms; UAE attacks tripled from 200,000 to 600,000 since war began. No CVEs. Threat actor: Iran state-directed.
items.recommended_action: Review SOC ML-based detection rules for AI-generated spear-phishing; brief security awareness team on AI-enhanced threat vectors before 1 June.

items.bold_heading: Hydro Kitten: Iran-linked actor with specific GCC financial sector targeting posture
items.text: Threat actor 'Hydro Kitten' (Iran-linked) has issued specific threats targeting the GCC financial services sector; methods include ransomware, wiper attacks, targeted data leaks, and defacements, accelerated by automation and AI integration.

items.bold_heading: CISA AA26-097a: IRGC OT/PLC exploitation — unchanged; kinetic escalation may activate contingency OT
items.text: IRGC-affiliated actors continue targeting Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley PLCs in critical infrastructure including data-centre energy management systems. IRGC kinetic-operational mode (confirmed 28 May) may activate pre-positioned OT contingency campaigns. Sector: critical infrastructure, energy management, Kuwait/Gulf.
items.assessed_likelihood: Low–Moderate (direct Kuwait OT); High (global IRGC OT campaign)

items.bold_heading: CISA AA26-113a: PRC pre-positioning in Gulf financial/energy infrastructure — unchanged
items.text: PRC-affiliated actors maintain low-level reconnaissance of Gulf financial, communications, and energy infrastructure; PRC-Iran strategic alignment sustains access maintenance incentive. No stand-down confirmed.
Trump's decision on MOU signing (~29–31 May)
items.why_it_matters: MOU signature triggers Hormuz de-mining clock, oil repricing ($85–88), OFAC sanctions waiver cascade, and war risk premium reassessment; rejection triggers re-escalation risk and Brent toward $100–110+.

items.text: Kuwait CAA confirmation on KWI T1 June 1 reopening
items.why_it_matters: CAA statement expected by 30 May following the May 28 airport area attack; absence of confirmation by 30 May should trigger June 1–3 travel schedule revisions for all staff.

items.text: Kuwait AQI trajectory — PM2.5 = 225 (Very Unhealthy); monitor through 1 June
items.why_it_matters: If PM2.5 remains above 150 (USG threshold) through 1 June, outdoor activity and facility re-entry protocols will need adjustment before the return-to-office date.

source_confidence.note: Kuwait MFA via CNBC/CNN corroborated; no new siren reports found in coverage window
